name: Wix
summary: Convenience-first launch path with hosting, templates, and editing all in one place.

name: Squarespace
summary: A polished visual platform for teams that want fewer moving parts and predictable upkeep.

name: Webflow CMS
summary: Useful when custom layouts matter and editors still need a friendly publishing workflow.

name: WordPress.org
summary: Flexible and extensible for teams that want control over content, plugins, and structure.

name: Ghost
summary: Good for blog-first or newsletter-style sites that need a focused editing experience.

name: GitHub Pages
summary: Very low-cost static hosting for simple content and a high-ownership launch path.

name: Cloudflare Pages
summary: Static hosting with strong DNS and CDN options for teams that want a bit more control.

name: Porkbun
summary: Usually the simplest low-cost registrar choice when price and privacy matter.

name: Namecheap
summary: A familiar registrar with easy onboarding and decent domain management tools.

name: Cloudflare Registrar
summary: Best when the team already understands DNS and wants a single control plane.

name: Tally
summary: A lightweight forms tool that fits many static and CMS-based launch paths.

name: Zeffy
summary: A useful default for nonprofit donations when keeping fees low matters most.

name: Stripe Checkout
summary: Reliable if the site needs payment collection for products, classes, or deposits.

name: Calendly
summary: A common choice for scheduling links and appointment flows.

name: TidyCal
summary: Often chosen when the team wants scheduling with a lower recurring bill.
